title: Subhash Chandra Bose Aapda Prabandhan Puraskar Recognising Excellence in Disaster Management
text:
Press Release:Press Information Bureau

Ministry of Home Affairs

Subhash Chandra Bose Aapda Prabandhan Puraskar Recognising Excellence in Disaster Management

Award seeks to highlight exemplary work and best practices that strengthen the country’s capacity to prevent, prepare for, respond to and recover from disasters
Nominations open throughout the year, applications must be submitted online through the National Awards Portal, self-nomination and third-party nomination permitted

Posted On:
23 MAR 2026 5:02PM by PIB Delhi

The Subhash Chandra Bose Aapda Prabandhan Puraskar is a prestigious National honour announced every year on 23
rd
 January, the birth anniversary of Subhash Chandra Bose, to recognise and acknowledge the invaluable contributions of individuals and institutions in the field of disaster management. Instituted by the Government of India, the award seeks to highlight exemplary work and best practices that strengthen the country’s capacity to prevent, prepare for, respond to and recover from disasters.

The award recognises outstanding contributions across the entire spectrum of disaster management, including prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, relief, rehabilitation, reconstruction, research, innovation, early warning systems, community awareness, and capacity building. It aims at encouraging innovation, promoting best practices, and inspiring individuals, organisations and institutions to contribute actively towards building a safer and disaster-resilient India. By celebrating exceptional efforts in disaster risk reduction and humanitarian response, the award also helps in disseminating successful models and motivating wider participation in strengthening national disaster resilience.

Award Categories

Individual
 – Scroll and Medal

Institution
 – Scroll and Plaque

Who Can Apply/Be Nominated?

Eligible nominees include

Individuals

Institutions/Organizations

National & State level entities

District authorities

Research & Academic institutions

NGOs & Civil Society Organisations

Private sector organisations

Both Indian citizens and institutions are eligible.

Nomination Process

Nominations are open throughout the year

Applications must be submitted online through the National Awards Portal
https://awards.gov.in


Self-nomination and third-party nomination are permitted





*****

RK/RR /PS

(Release ID: 2244009)

Visitor Counter : 122
